title: Maharashtra Launches AI Policy 2026 and Wins MLC Seats Unopposed
summary: The Fadnavis government unveiled an Artificial Intelligence Policy 2026 aimed at attracting tech investment. Separately, six Mahayuti candidates won Maharashtra Legislative Council seats unopposed, with Deputy CM Shinde predicting a clean sweep of all 17 alliance candidates in the upcoming biennial polls.

coverages.headline: Maharashtra Government Announces AI Policy 2026 to Boost Investment and Jobs
coverages.excerpt: The Fadnavis government has unveiled a new Artificial Intelligence Policy 2026, aimed at attracting technology investment and generating skilled employment across the state.
coverages.bias_notes: Government broadcaster covers government policy announcement — a structural alignment between the source and the story. 'Aimed at' is appropriately hedged language since the policy's actual outcomes are unknown. What's absent: any independent technology expert assessment of whether the policy is ambitious, realistic, or distinguishable from similar policies announced by other states.

coverages.headline: Mahayuti Wins Thane-Palghar MLC Seat Unopposed; Shinde Calls It 'Beginning of Clean Sweep'
coverages.excerpt: Six Mahayuti candidates have been elected unopposed to the Maharashtra Legislative Council; Deputy CM Eknath Shinde says all 17 alliance candidates will win in the upcoming biennial council polls.
coverages.bias_notes: Winning a seat 'unopposed' is often less a sign of political strength than of an opposition too weak or too divided to contest. The headline frames it as a triumph; the political context — why no opposition candidate stood — is not explored. 'Clean sweep' is Shinde's own language, quoted without examination of whether the biennial polls will actually deliver that result.
